text:
Most airlines are partnering with @Starlink. 

The others will have terrible WiFi and lose customers as a result. https://t.co/rjqVSZSarY
summary: Most airlines are partnering with @Starlink. The others will have terrible WiFi and lose customers as a result.
ai_summary: Elon Musk claims that airlines adopting Starlink for in-flight connectivity will gain competitive advantage over competitors offering inferior WiFi, creating customer churn pressure. This illustrates a key business strategy lesson: companies that secure superior technology partnerships can differentiate on customer experience and win market share, particularly in industries where service quality directly impacts customer retention. The insight applies to Starlink's B2B expansion strategy and how technology infrastructure partnerships influence competitive dynamics across industries.
embed_html:
Most airlines are partnering with @Starlink.

The others will have terrible WiFi and lose customers as a result. https://t.co/rjqVSZSarY

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 15, 2026

text:
The latest 𝕏 algorithm has been published to GitHub
https://t.co/ZCOm51uxmh
summary: The latest 𝕏 algorithm has been published to GitHub
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that X (formerly Twitter) has open-sourced its recommendation algorithm on GitHub, making the codebase publicly available for scrutiny and community contribution. This demonstrates X's commitment to algorithmic transparency and represents a significant product/platform decision that allows developers and researchers to understand how content ranking works, potentially enabling better third-party integrations and rebuilding trust through technical openness—a notable business and engineering strategy for a major social media platform.
embed_html:
The latest 𝕏 algorithm has been published to GitHubhttps://t.co/ZCOm51uxmh

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 15, 2026

text:
Critique of the 𝕏 algorithm is welcome. 

There will be monthly updates of the latest algorithm to GitHub with release notes. 

As reminder, you can always choose no algorithm via the Following tab. https://t.co/jBj6uGNgeq
summary: Critique of the 𝕏 algorithm is welcome. There will be monthly updates of the latest algorithm to GitHub with release notes. As reminder, you can always choose no algorithm via the Following tab.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that X (formerly Twitter) will publish monthly algorithm updates to GitHub with release notes, inviting public critique while reminding users they can opt into a non-algorithmic feed via the Following tab. This demonstrates a product transparency and user control strategy for a major social media platform, reflecting how companies can build trust through open-source practices and user choice in algorithmic curation.

text: Go in with expectations that Grok Build is still beta, but improving almost every day https://t.co/U9xG8LCPtQ
summary: Go in with expectations that Grok Build is still beta, but improving almost every day
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Grok Build, xAI's AI development tool, remains in beta but is experiencing rapid daily improvements. This reflects xAI's product development strategy of releasing tools early while in active development cycles, demonstrating the iterative approach to shipping AI products and managing user expectations during the scaling phase of a new AI company's offerings.

text:
The goal of the 𝕏 algorithm is simple: show each user the content they are most likely to find interesting, limited only by the laws within a given jurisdiction. No thumb on the scale. 

Doesn’t mean we achieve the goal, but that is what I tell the team.
summary: The goal of the 𝕏 algorithm is simple: show each user the content they are most likely to find interesting, limited only by the laws within a given jurisdiction. No thumb on the scale. Doesn’t mean we achieve the goal, but that is what I tell the team.

ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that X's recommendation algorithm will receive monthly updates published to GitHub with release notes, and clarifies the algorithm's core objective: showing users content most likely to interest them within legal boundaries, without editorial bias. This demonstrates a product strategy focused on algorithmic transparency, user choice (via the Following tab alternative), and engineering accountability—key operational practices for platforms balancing engagement with user control.
